verdicts.note: Constitutional limits and government accountability do not, without a budget or spending position, locate him on the spending axis.
Six years as a county commissioner and national county leadership establish a proven local record, but not the maximal federal institutional record represented by -2.
Law enforcement is an explicit priority, but the dossier documents no detailed enforcement proposal warranting maximal strength.
Gun rights do not map to the rubric's traditional-values and parental-rights pole, and the dossier documents no other specific social-policy position.
Public-land protection alone does not establish a position on carbon pricing, clean-energy transition, or climate-versus-affordability policy.
A general emphasis on family and church is not a documented position on reproductive rights, LGBTQ protections, curricula, or parental control.
Independence from party leadership and cross-partisan work do not by themselves support the rubric's structural money-and-power reforms.
Anti-establishment framing is not a specific structural reform, campaign-finance position, or institutional-money practice.
Criticism of costly wars and Gaza policy supports the direction, but the dossier documents no categorical military-spending cut or specific aid condition warranting +2.

His tax proposals restore the top bracket, cap deductions, and increase the Medicare payroll tax. His other restraint proposals concern spending, not offsetting tax cuts, so the tax position leans toward added revenue rather than genuinely mixed.
Faith and family language alone does not document a position on the rubric's social-policy questions.
Less regulation is not a spending position, and the dossier provides no budget-restraint, audit, or spending proposal.

review_notes: All 52 candidate-axis scores were reviewed. The retained scores use only Federal-applicable axes and source ids present in each dossier. Generic issue mentions without a rubric mechanism were not proposed as missing scores.
